title: AutoCommandX
description: Bind up to 4 hotkeys to send any chat text or command (as typed) on any server.
body:
**💬 Description**

Stop wasting time typing the same commands over and over!
Auto Command (Client) lets you bind up to four custom hotkeys to instantly send any chat message or command — perfect for quick teleports, server macros, or role-playing emotes. It’s completely client-side, so it works on any server without needing admin permissions or server mods.

**✨ Features**

- ⚡ Instant Chat/Command Hotkeys — bind up to 4 custom keys for your favorite commands.
- 💬 Sends Exactly What You Type — works with both /commands and regular chat messages.
- 🎛️ In-Game Setup — configure everything directly from chat.
- 💾 Auto-Saves — commands persist across sessions in config/autocommand.json.
- 🧱 No Server Mod Needed — works seamlessly on vanilla, modded, and multiplayer servers.
- 🎨 Lightweight & Silent — runs quietly with zero lag or chat spam.

**🧩 Commands**

/autocmd set <1-4> <text>
→ Sets the command or message for the chosen slot.

Example:
```
/autocmd set 1 /home
/autocmd set 2 /warp spawn
```
/autocmd list
→ Shows all current slots and their assigned commands.

Example output:
```
[AutoCommand] 1: /home
[AutoCommand] 2: /warp spawn
[AutoCommand] 3: <empty>
[AutoCommand] 4: <empty>
```
/autocmd clear <1-4>
→ Removes the command assigned to that slot.

Example:
```
/autocmd clear 1
```
After setting your commands, go to:
Options → Controls → Key Binds → Miscellaneous → Auto Command 1-4
Bind each to any key (e.g., P, O, I, U).
Press the key anytime to run your command instantly.

**🧱 Requirements**

- Fabric Loader: 0.17.3 +
- Minecraft: 1.21.9 (also compatible with 1.21.10)
- Fabric API: Latest for 1.21.9

**📦 Optional Mods (Recommended)**

- Mod Menu – view mod icon and description in-game.
- YetAnotherConfigLib (YACL) – planned GUI editor for easier command setup.

**🧠 Notes**

Auto Command (Client) is a pure quality-of-life tool.
It doesn’t bypass server permissions or alter gameplay — it simply automates what you already type. Perfect for PvP macros, /home warps, or quick team messages.
